scope: Migrate sworn's Go status.json carriers (open_deferrals, verification.violations) up to the slice-status-v1 object shape so the autonomous loop reads real coach-produced status without dying on the D6 unmarshal, round-trips it loss-free and byte-stably, reconciles need_ids->covers_needs, and adds the inconclusive result enum + acknowledged_by write-back tolerance.

test_results.output: 39 packages ok, 0 FAIL, 0 panic, no hang (per-package 120s timeout)

8/10 applicable checks PASS (status valid, state implemented, 24-file diff, dark-code clean). The 2 FAILs are markdown-era artefact-name drift in the generic ~/.claude release-verify.sh: it looks for spec.md/proof.md, but this records-as-JSON release uses spec.json/proof.json (present + proof.json validates against proof-v1 via baton.Validate AND baton.ValidateSchema). Harness drift, not a slice defect.

reachability_artifacts: manual-smoke-step (AC-08), non-destructive against the live fired repo (~/projects/fired; git clean before AND after — Rule 11 restore verified). The board oracle and RunSlice share the exact state.Read path that crashed the original dogfood run ('RunSlice: read status: cannot unmarshal object into Go struct field Status.open_deferrals of type string').

BEFORE/AFTER on REAL object-form data: `sworn board --release 2026-06-16-critical-journey-resolutions`, slice S05-engine-owned-real-dollars (4 object-form open_deferrals) — OLD installed binary: 'S05-engine-owned-real-dollars — unknown' (state.Read swallowed the D6 unmarshal failure); NEW binary: 'S05-engine-owned-real-dollars — verified [verifier]' (object form parsed, true state recovered). Artefact: reachability/ac08-reachability.txt.

DIRECT state.Read (the exact failing line) on the AC-08 target slice S01-networth-hierarchy-remap AND the real 4-deferral S05 status.json: both read without error; S05 why/tracking parse into named fields and unknown coach keys (acknowledged_by, acknowledged_at, field, id) are preserved in Extra — AC-01/AC-03/AC-10 confirmed on live data (real deferrals carry acknowledged_by and NO acknowledgement). Artefact: reachability/ac08-direct-read.txt.

NEW-binary full `sworn board` output greps to 0 occurrences of 'cannot unmarshal object'. NOTE: the AC-08 target release's board.json carries a SEPARATE out-of-scope sibling bug (BoardRecord.release as object, design.md Risk #4) that does not affect the slice status-read path under test.
delivered: AC-01: state.Read unmarshals fired's real object-form open_deferrals + violations without error — state.go Deferral/Violation UnmarshalJSON; TestRead_ObjectFormDeferralsAndViolations_NoUnmarshalError; live ac08-direct-read.txt (S05).
AC-02: read->write->read->write preserves every field (incl. unknown keys) and is byte-stable (map-based MarshalJSON, sorted keys) — TestRoundTrip_PreservesFieldsAndIsByteStable.

AC-03: OpenDeferrals []Deferral and Verification.Violations []Violation are typed structs with an Extra overflow map preserving unknown keys — TestRead_TypedStructsPreserveUnknownKeys; live Extra=[acknowledged_at acknowledged_by field id] on S05.

AC-04: display consumers read via DeferralStrings()/ViolationStrings(); oracle SliceState.Violations stays []string; unrelated report types untouched — oracle.go:236/259, ledger.go:157, implement.go:187, proof_record.go:74; router/route/validate_blocked unchanged.

AC-05: CheckBoundaryMocks/isDeclared take []state.Deferral, match Item+Why only, stay at least as strict — TestCheckBoundaryMocks_DeclaredViaObjectWhyPasses, _KeywordInTrackingDoesNotOverDeclare, _UndeclaredDbMockFails.
AC-06: state.Status NeedIDs->CoversNeeds (need_ids->covers_needs); writers spec_record.go + task.go updated — TestWriteSpecRecord_ParsesSpecAndWritesJSON.

AC-07: slice-status-v1 verification.result enum gains 'inconclusive'; merge gate keys on state not result so it can never pass — TestSchema_InconclusiveResultValidates; merge.go:105. Closes the AC-07 half of #37.
AC-08: the exact RunSlice status-read path succeeds on the live fired repo; previously-unreadable real object slice now reads its true state — reachability_artifacts above.
AC-09: go build ./... and go test ./... pass (39 packages ok, 0 FAIL, no hang) — test_results 1-2.

AC-10: open_deferrals required-set relaxed to anyOf[acknowledgement | acknowledged_by]; write-back of real acknowledged_by-only deferral succeeds; neither-key deferral fails closed — slice-status-v1.json anyOf; TestWrite_AcknowledgedByOnlyDeferral_NoError, TestSchema_OpenDeferralAcknowledgementAnyOf.

Backward-compat: legacy string-form open_deferrals/violations still parse (one-way upgrade to object on write-back) so existing on-disk boards keep working — state.go UnmarshalJSON string branch; board oracle_test legacy fixtures pass.
divergence: AC-10 write-path enforcement reading: live state.Write calls the legacy structural baton.Validate (never evaluates open_deferrals), not draft-2020-12 baton.ValidateSchema. The wholesale Validate->ValidateSchema rewire is the deferred keystone step-1b follow-up (validate_schema.go comment) and, measured here, would break out-of-AC writes (task.go covers_needs ['N/A-task-mode'] vs ^N-\d+$; defer_slice has no tracking). AC-10 satisfied via schema anyOf (load-bearing) + write-path positive test + schema-level negative test; the wholesale rewire stays the named follow-up, filed as #39. Most faithful in-scope reading; avoids an out-of-AC architecturally-significant change.

Touchpoints subset: of the 16 planned touchpoints, internal/verify/validate_blocked.go (len-only on Violations), internal/router/router.go and cmd/sworn/route.go (consume the oracle SliceState.Violations which stays []string by D2) needed no change — design predicted validate_blocked.go would be len-only. Fewer files than planned, none outside the track.

Legacy string-form read tolerance added to the unmarshalers (not explicitly in the AC set) because the migration would otherwise make sworn unable to read its own previously-written string-form status.json — a regression for every existing board, contrary to the operational-readiness goal. The design's 'no flatten-to-string read tolerance' constraint is about write-back (still always object form); upgrading legacy string->object on read does not conflict.

Proof-bundle gate format: the generic ~/.claude release-verify.sh is markdown-era (expects spec.md/proof.md); this records-as-JSON release uses spec.json/proof.json. All applicable structural checks pass; proof.json validates against proof-v1 (baton.Validate + baton.ValidateSchema).
